varieties.name: Mexican Tarragon (Standard)
varieties.origin: Mexico and Central America, traditional cultivation since Aztec times (pre-1500s)
varieties.traits: Bushy perennial herb, 60-90cm tall, 30-45cm wide. Bright green, narrow, oblong leaves (7.5cm long). Golden-yellow, five-petaled marigold-like flowers in clusters, blooming profusely late summer to autumn.
varieties.yield_notes: High productivity. Continuous harvest spring through autumn. Can produce year-round in frost-free Brisbane winters.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (Australia-wide delivery including QLD), available as seeds. Quality Plants & Seedlings (express delivery to QLD). Plantmark Wholesale Nursery. Can be grown from seed sown after last frost or started indoors.

varieties.name: Mexican Mint Marigold
varieties.origin: Same as Mexican Tarragon (alternative common name)
varieties.traits: Identical to Mexican Tarragon. Upright growth habit, can reach up to 36 inches (90cm) in warm climates with woody stems.
varieties.availability: Same suppliers as Mexican Tarragon (same plant, different name).

varieties.name: Texas Tarragon / Winter Tarragon
varieties.traits: Regional name for Mexican Tarragon. Dark green, narrow leaves with slight shine. Grows 60-91cm tall.
varieties.yield_notes: Very productive, year-round in mild winters.
varieties.availability: Australian seed suppliers including The Seed Collection.

varieties.name: French Tarragon (True)
varieties.origin: Southern Europe and Central Asia, cultivated since medieval times (1200s-1300s). In English gardens since 16th century. Brought to America by colonists. Thomas Jefferson grew it at Monticello.
varieties.traits: Herbaceous perennial, 40-60cm tall (can reach 120-150cm). Slender branches, narrow lanceolate grey-green leaves (2-8cm long, 2-10mm wide). Rarely flowers; small greenish-yellow sterile flowers when present. Spreads via rhizomes.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate. Best harvested spring and early summer in Brisbane. Dies back in heat.
varieties.availability: Mudbrick Herb Cottage (organic, ships to QLD). White House Nursery (ships to QLD). Quality Plants & Seedlings (express delivery to QLD). Allrareherbs (online). Must be purchased as live plants or cuttings, never seeds.

varieties.name: Russian Tarragon
varieties.traits: Vigorous perennial, grows over 1 meter tall. Coarser, thicker leaves than French variety. Produces flowers and viable seeds (unlike French).
varieties.yield_notes: Very high, but poor flavor quality.
varieties.availability: The Seed Collection (seeds - but not recommended for purchase). Any commercial 'tarragon seeds' are Russian tarragon.

varieties.name: Mexican Tarragon (Tropical Strain)
varieties.origin: Native to tropical Mexico, Guatemala, and Central America
varieties.traits: Tropical perennial herb, 60-90cm tall (can reach 1m in optimal tropical conditions). Bushy, branching growth. Narrow, shiny medium-green leaves. Abundant golden-yellow flowers.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent year-round production in Kerala's tropical climate. Continuous harvest possible.
varieties.availability: GardenGuru.in (Kerala delivery), Ugaoo (all-India delivery including Kerala), GreenMyLife (ships to Kerala), PlantsBazar.com (India-wide), local Kerala nurseries. Available as seeds or plants.

varieties.name: Yerba Anís / Pericón
varieties.origin: Traditional Mexican and Central American herb, same species as Mexican Tarragon
varieties.traits: Traditional strain used medicinally and in cooking. Identical botanical characteristics to Mexican Tarragon.
varieties.availability: GreenMyLife (Kerala delivery), specialty herb suppliers in India.

varieties.name: Sweet Mace / Mexican Marigold
varieties.traits: Same plant as Mexican Tarragon with emphasis on ornamental yellow flowers.
varieties.availability: Indian seed suppliers, online nurseries delivering to Kerala.

varieties.name: French Tarragon 🏆 ❌
varieties.traits: Temperate climate herb requiring cool winters and dry summers.

varieties.name: Russian Tarragon 🌱 ❌
varieties.availability: Seeds available in India (Ugaoo, GardenGuru, PlantsBazar) but not recommended for purchase.
